ONCOLOGY INITIAL CONSULTATION NOTE
Date: 2016-06-13
Patient: Maldonado, Michelle | DOB: 1949-09 | MRN: pat_044657a8
Practice: prac_0006

REASON FOR CONSULTATION:
New diagnosis of advanced non-small cell lung cancer, stage IIIC.

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Michelle Maldonado is a 67-year-old female with a 75-pack-year smoking history
who presents with a new diagnosis of adenocarcinoma of the lung, stage IIIC per
AJCC 8th edition. Initial symptoms included progressive dyspnea and a 6-week
history of hemoptysis. CT of the chest revealed a right lower lobe mass with mediastinal
lymphadenopathy. PET/CT confirmed IIIC disease.

PERFORMANCE STATUS:
ECOG performance status: 2

MOLECULAR TESTING:
EGFR: negative; ALK: negative; ROS1: negative; KRAS: positive; BRAF: positive; PD-L1: negative (TPS 0.9%)

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
Diagnosis: adenocarcinoma, stage IIIC, progression from earlier stage.
Will initiate carboplatin + pemetrexed + pembrolizumab per guideline-concordant approach.

Follow-up in 6 weeks for treatment monitoring.

SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT
Accession Number: PATH-pat_044657a8
Specimen Date: 2016-06-13
Patient: Maldonado, Michelle | MRN: pat_044657a8

CLINICAL HISTORY:
Right upper lobe mass, suspicious for malignancy. Rule out primary lung carcinoma.

SPECIMENS SUBMITTED:
A. CT-guided core needle biopsy, right upper lobe mass (3 cores)
B. Bronchial washings

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
A. Three tan-white cores measuring 0.5-1.2 cm in aggregate length.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show fragments of lung parenchyma with infiltrating carcinoma.
The tumor demonstrates glandular architecture with mucin production, consistent with adenocarcinoma..

DIAGNOSIS:
A-B. Invasive adenocarcinoma of the lung, 8140/3
   - Stage at diagnosis: IIIC (AJCC 8th edition)
   - Lymphovascular invasion: present
   - Ki-67 proliferative index: approximately 35%

MOLECULAR ANCILLARY STUDIES:
EGFR: negative; ALK: negative; ROS1: negative; KRAS: positive; BRAF: positive; PD-L1: negative (TPS 0.9%)

COMMENT:
This case was reviewed at multidisciplinary tumor board. Molecular testing was recommended per NCCN guidelines for advanced NSCLC.

Electronically signed: Synthetic Pathologist, MD

ONCOLOGY PROGRESS NOTE
Date: 2016-09-26
Patient: Maldonado, Michelle | MRN: pat_044657a8

SUBJECTIVE:
Patient returns for cycle 5 of carboplatin, pemetrexed, pembrolizumab. Patient reports mild fatigue, grade 1. No nausea or vomiting.

OBJECTIVE:
ECOG performance status: 2
Weight: 91.6 kg
Laboratory values within acceptable parameters for treatment continuation.

ASSESSMENT:
adenocarcinoma NSCLC, stage IIIC.
Treatment: carboplatin, pemetrexed, pembrolizumab (initiated 2016-07-19).
CT imaging shows stable disease. No new lesions identified.

PLAN:
Disease progression identified. Discontinue current carboplatin, pemetrexed, pembrolizumab. Referral to clinical trial team. Consider second-line therapy options.

Disease progression documented on 2017-04-28 with new involvement at: liver, brain, adrenal.

RADIOLOGY REPORT — CT CHEST WITH CONTRAST
Study Date: 2016-06-13
Patient: Maldonado, Michelle | MRN: pat_044657a8
Ordering Provider: Medical Oncology

INDICATION:
adenocarcinoma lung cancer, stage IIIC. Staging evaluation.

COMPARISON:
No prior CT available.

TECHNIQUE:
Helical CT acquisition through the chest with IV contrast.

FINDINGS:
Primary tumor: left lower lobe mass measuring 5.4 cm in greatest dimension,
with spiculated margins. No evidence of chest wall invasion.

Mediastinum/Hilar: Enlarged mediastinal lymph nodes measuring up to 1.8 cm,
most prominent in the right paratracheal station (4R). Bilateral mediastinal adenopathy raising concern for N2 or N3 nodal involvement.

Pleural spaces: No pleural effusion.

Pulmonary vasculature: Normal.

Bones: No bone lesions identified on current study.

IMPRESSION:
1. 5.4 cm left lower lobe primary lung mass consistent with known adenocarcinoma,
   stage IIIC.
2. Mediastinal lymphadenopathy as described above.
3. Pleural effusion as above, may warrant thoracentesis.

Disease progression documented on 2017-04-28 with new involvement at: liver, brain, adrenal.

Practice: prac_0001

REASON FOR CONSULTATION:
New diagnosis of advanced non-small cell lung cancer, stage IVB.

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Robert Peterson is a 71-year-old male with a 8-pack-year smoking history
who presents with a new diagnosis of squamous cell carcinoma of the lung, stage IVB per
AJCC 8th edition. Initial symptoms included progressive dyspnea and a 6-week
history of hemoptysis. CT of the chest revealed a right lower lobe mass with mediastinal
lymphadenopathy. PET/CT confirmed IVB disease.

PERFORMANCE STATUS:
ECOG performance status: 1

MOLECULAR TESTING:
EGFR: negative; ALK: negative; ROS1: negative; KRAS: negative; BRAF: negative

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
Diagnosis: squamous cell carcinoma, stage IVB, de novo stage iiib plus.
Will initiate carboplatin + paclitaxel + pembrolizumab per guideline-concordant approach.

Follow-up in 3 weeks for treatment monitoring.

Patient: Peterson, Robert | MRN: pat_0b01ea9d
